notes: MITRE develops and maintains several foundational security standards and specifications beyond their vulnerability/threat databases. This reference entry covers MITRE-originated specifications and standards. See also: advisory/mitre.org (CVE), weakness/mitre.org (CWE), ttp/mitre.org (ATT&CK, ATLAS, CAPEC), control/mitre.org, entity/mitre.org, disclosure/mitre.org.

data.description: Standardized naming scheme for IT products and platforms.
data.notes: Used in NVD to identify affected products. CPE 2.3 is current. Format: cpe:2.3:part:vendor:product:version:... Maintained as a NIST dictionary.

data.note: CPE strings use colon-delimited fields. Part types: a (application), h (hardware), o (operating system).

data.description: Structured language for describing cyber threat intelligence.
data.notes: STIX 2.1 is current. JSON-based format for sharing threat intelligence — indicators, attack patterns, campaigns, threat actors. Now maintained by OASIS. Transported via TAXII protocol.

match_nodes.description: TAXII — Trusted Automated Exchange of Intelligence Information

data.official_name: Trusted Automated Exchange of Intelligence Information
data.description: Transport protocol for sharing cyber threat intelligence.
data.notes: TAXII 2.1 is current. HTTP-based protocol for exchanging STIX content. Defines collections, channels, and API endpoints. Now maintained by OASIS.

match_nodes.description: MAEC — Malware Attribute Enumeration and Characterization

data.description: Standardized language for encoding and communicating malware attributes.
data.notes: MAEC 5.0 is current. Provides a structured way to describe malware behavior, artifacts, and relationships. Less widely adopted than STIX but useful for malware analysis.

match_nodes.description: OVAL — Open Vulnerability and Assessment Language

data.description: Language for determining vulnerability presence and system configuration.
data.notes: Community standard for assessing and reporting on machine state. Used in SCAP (Security Content Automation Protocol). Defines tests for checking system configurations.
